title: 帷幕
summary:
Following a heated argument where Judith declares that a specific man means everything to her, she expresses intense anger toward Hastings for interfering in her life and retreats. Feeling disappointed and helpless, Hastings is joined by Elizabeth Coleridge and Norton. During a walk to observe nature, Norton uses a telescope to look at a distant grove of trees but suddenly stops, appearing pale, anxious, and bewildered. When Hastings demands to see through the telescope, Norton attempts to claim he merely misidentified a bird. Hastings snatches the telescope and sees only a flash of white, possibly a girl's dress, disappearing into the trees.

Upon returning to the villa, Hastings encounters Mrs. Franklin and Boyd Carrington. Mrs. Franklin, appearing radiant after a shopping trip to Tedcastle, discusses the local drought and expresses concern regarding John’s recent headaches and his worries about an experiment. She also questions Norton’s frightened appearance. Shortly after, Curtis wheels Polo through the doorway; Polo appears alert and asks what is happening, but Mrs. Franklin dismisses the tension by attributing it to an approaching storm.

Hastings follows Mrs. Franklin to her room in the east wing, where Nurse Craven is examining Boyd Carrington’s hand. After Mrs. Franklin requests service and Boyd departs, Hastings heads toward the room shared with Poirot. Outside Alton's room, Hastings encounters Judith. He drags her into a room to confront her about visiting Alton, calling him "scum." Judith responds with indifference and sarcasm, stating she does not view him as a saint, while Hastings continues to protest her contact with him.
